ddls:
CREATE TABLE "server_performance" (
  "performance_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each performance metric record */,
  "server_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the server from which the data was collected */,
  "timestamp_ms" TEXT /* Timestamp in milliseconds when the data was collected */,
  "guest" REAL /* Percentage of CPU usage by guest OS */,
  "idle" REAL /* Percentage of CPU usage by idle processes */,
  "iowait" REAL /* Percentage of CPU usage waiting for I/O operations */,
  "irq" REAL /* Percentage of CPU usage by hardware interrupts */,
  "nice" REAL /* Percentage of CPU usage by user processes with nice priority */,
  "softirq" REAL /* Percentage of CPU usage by software interrupts */,
  "steal" REAL /* Percentage of CPU usage by virtualization steal time */,
  "sys" REAL /* Percentage of CPU usage by system processes */,
  "usr" REAL /* Percentage of CPU usage by user processes */,
  "metric_version" TEXT /* Version of the metric */,
  "collected_by" TEXT /* User or system that collected the data */,
  "data_source" TEXT /* Source of the data (e.g., agent, API) */,
  "metric_status" TEXT /* Status of the metric (e.g., active, deprecated) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("performance_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_server_performance_server_id FOREIGN KEY ("server_id") REFERENCES servers ("server_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "servers" (
  "server_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each server */,
  "server_name" TEXT /* Name of the server */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address of the server */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location of the server */,
  "data_center_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the data center where the server is located */,
  "server_type_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the type of the server (e.g., web server, database server) */,
  "os_type" TEXT /* Operating system type (e.g., Linux, Windows) */,
  "os_version" TEXT /* Operating system version */,
  "hardware_config" TEXT /* Hardware configuration details */,
  "last_reboot" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last server reboot */,
  "last_update" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last server configuration update */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("server_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_servers_server_type_id FOREIGN KEY ("server_type_id") REFERENCES server_types ("server_type_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_servers_data_center_id FOREIGN KEY ("data_center_id") REFERENCES data_centers ("data_center_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_centers" (
  "data_center_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data center */,
  "data_center_name" TEXT /* Name of the data center */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location of the data center */,
  "contact_email" TEXT /* Contact email for inquiries about the data center */,
  "manager_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the user responsible for managing the data center */,
  "availability_zone" TEXT /* Availability zone of the data center */,
  "security_level" TEXT /* Security level of the data center */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("data_center_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_centers_manager_id FOREIGN KEY ("manager_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "server_types" (
  "server_type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each server type */,
  "server_type_name" TEXT /* Name of the server type */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the server type */,
  "default_metrics" TEXT /* Default performance metrics for this server type */,
  "max_cpu_usage" REAL /* Maximum acceptable CPU usage for this server type */,
  "min_memory" REAL /* Minimum acceptable memory usage for this server type */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("server_type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "performance_metrics" (
  "metric_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each performance metric */,
  "metric_name" TEXT /* Name of the performance metric */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the performance metric */,
  "metric_unit" TEXT /* Unit of the metric (e.g., percentage, MB) */,
  "metric_threshold" REAL /* Threshold value for the metric */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("metric_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., IT administrator, data analyst, system engineer) */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department of the user */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number for contact */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the user account was created */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last user login */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "performance_id" INTEGER /* ID of the performance metric record being accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the performance data */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the performance data was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, download) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address from which the access was made */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent of the access device */,
  "access_duration" REAL /* Duration of the access event in seconds */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_performance_id FOREIGN KEY ("performance_id") REFERENCES server_performance ("performance_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "performance_reports" (
  "report_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each report */,
  "report_name" TEXT /* Name of the report */,
  "report_type" TEXT /* Type of the report (e.g., daily, weekly) */,
  "report_date" TEXT /* Date the report was generated */,
  "file_path" TEXT /* File path to the report */,
  "file_size" REAL /* Size of the report file in MB */,
  "created_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who generated the report */,
  "report_status" TEXT /* Status of the report (e.g., draft, published) */,
  "report_description" TEXT /* Description of the report */,
  "last_modified" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last modification */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("report_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_performance_reports_created_by FOREIGN KEY ("created_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "report_access" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "report_id" INTEGER /* ID of the report being accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the report */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the report was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, download) */,
  "access_duration" REAL /* Duration of the access event in seconds */,
  "access_ip" TEXT /* IP address from which the report was accessed */,
  "access_user_agent" TEXT /* User agent of the access device */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_report_access_report_id FOREIGN KEY ("report_id") REFERENCES performance_reports ("report_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_report_access_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "alert_logs" (
  "alert_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each alert */,
  "performance_id" INTEGER /* ID of the performance metric record that triggered the alert */,
  "alert_type" TEXT /* Type of the alert (e.g., high CPU usage, low memory) */,
  "alert_message" TEXT /* Message of the alert */,
  "alert_date" TEXT /* Date when the alert was generated */,
  "resolved" INTEGER /* Flag indicating whether the alert has been resolved */,
  "alert_severity" TEXT /* Severity of the alert (e.g., low, medium, high) */,
  "alert_source" TEXT /* Source of the alert (e.g., system, user) */,
  "alert_resolved_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who resolved the alert */,
  "resolution_notes" TEXT /* Notes on how the alert was resolved */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("alert_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_alert_logs_performance_id FOREIGN KEY ("performance_id") REFERENCES server_performance ("performance_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_alert_logs_alert_resolved_by FOREIGN KEY ("alert_resolved_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)
